items.summary: The source of cyclosporiasis outbreaks is still unknown but fresh produce is suspected.

items.title: Man fatally shot by ICE officer during traffic stop in Houston
items.summary: ICE officers were trying to arrest Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, who the Department of Homeland Security said isn’t authorized to be in the country.

items.title: The best fitness trackers of 2026, tested and reviewed
items.summary: I’ve tested dozens of fitness trackers from Apple, Garmin, Fitbit and more. Here are my top picks for beginners, runners and anyone on a budget.

items.title: Attorneys for Karmelo Anthony appeal his murder conviction in track meet stabbing
items.summary: The attorneys say Anthony, convicted of fatally stabbing fellow student-athlete Austin Metcalf, was denied his constitutional rights; they also asked that the judge be removed from the case.

items.title: McConnell speaks to GOP leaders as speculation swirls about health, hospitalization
items.summary: Senate Republican leaders have spoken to Kentucky Sen. Mitch McConnell as he remains in the hospital

items.title: Suiza enciende sus motores para el duelo ante Colombia en octavos de final
items.summary: Los helvéticos se presentan para enfrentar un duelo importante ante la Colombia de Nestor Lorenzo que ha jugado de gran forma en la Copa Mundial. ¿Quién se llevará el boleto a cuartos?

items.title: Colombia calienta motores antes de un choque crucial por el boleto a cuartos
items.summary: Colombia afina detalles en la cancha para enfrentarse al siempre ordenado conjunto suizo, en un partido definitivo que determinará si el equipo de James Rodríguez, Luis Díaz y el entrenador Néstor Lorenzo logra avanzar a cuartos de final.

items.title: Messi: “Este equipo no baja los brazos nunca”
items.summary: Lionel celebró la gran remontada de Argentina sobre Egipto, donde la ‘Albiceleste’ estaba abajo 2-0 y terminó ganando el partido 2-3 sobre la hora. El capitán aseguró que así serán todos los partidos y que su equipo siempre luchará hasta el final.

items.title: ¡Misión imposible! Suiza llega a Vancouver para dar la sorpresa a Luis Díaz y Colombia
items.summary: Los helvéticos se presentan para enfrentar un duelo importante ante la Colombia de Nestor Lorenzo que ha jugado de gran forma en la Copa Mundial. ¿Quién se llevará el boleto a cuartos?

items.title: Murat Yakin habla sobre los cambios que hizo al equipo suizo para enfrentar a Colombia
items.summary: Yakin explicó la inclusión de Fabian Rieder y Ardon Jashari para el juego decisivo de octavos de final ante la selección de Colombia, en el que no podrá contar con una de sus estrellas, Manzambi, lesionado en el último entrenamiento.

items.title: Freuler: "Fue un shock la lesión de Manzambi, pero tenemos suficientes jugadores"
items.summary: Titular con la selección de Suiza para enfrentar a Colombia, Remo Freuler habló sobre lo que significó para el grupo la lesión de Johan Manzambi, y aseguró que la plantilla lo va a gestionar de la mejor manera.

items.title: Néstor Lorenzo y el sueño de Colombia: “Queremos seguir alimentando esta ilusión”
items.summary: Néstor Lorenzo habló antes del duelo ante Suiza por los octavos de final de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026 y reveló el mensaje que le dejó al equipo: seguir alimentando la ilusión de todo un país y dejarlo todo en la cancha.

items.title: Julián Álvarez: “Se está viendo en esta Copa del Mundo que todos los partidos son muy parejos”
items.summary: El delantero del Atlético de Madrid mencionó que el equipo egipcio tiene su mérito por lograr anotar antes pero celebra el control de juego de la selección de Argentina y la seguridad a la hora de meter goles.

items.title: Reigning champion Argentina escapes with remarkable World Cup victory over Egypt
items.summary: Egypt was leading late, up 2-0. The Argentinians looked beaten. But they fought and fought and fought. Scoring one goal, then another to equalize. And, finally, a third to advance to the quarterfinal.

items.title: Lautaro Martínez: “Fue un partido que queda para el recuerdo”
items.summary: El delantero del Inter de Milán reconoce la dificultad que tuvieron en el encuentro ante Egipto, señala las claves del éxito argentino y el orgullo de representar a la nación.

items.title: Argentina resucita con Messi y elimina a Egipto.
items.summary: Cuando todo parecía perdido para Argentina, Egipto estuvo muy cerca de dar el gran golpe del Mundial. Sin embargo, Messi lideró una espectacular remontada para revertir un 2-0 que parecía sentenciar el partido. ¡Partidazo!

items.title: Former NBA star Lamar Odom expected to enter plea deal in Las Vegas DUI case
items.summary: Former NBA star Lamar Odom is expected to enter a plea deal in a DUI case in Las Vegas

items.title: U.S. shared confidential immigration details with Iranian government, lawsuit alleges
items.summary: The Iranian American Legal Defense Fund stated that the alleged sharing of information violates asylum seekers’ confidentiality and endangers them and their family members.

items.title: Growing number of Democrats rescind endorsements of Graham Platner
items.summary: A growing number of Democrats are rescinding their endorsement of Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner after a sexual assault allegation. NBC News' Melanie Zanona reports on what Platner has had to say and what could happen if he were to exit the race.

items.title: Enzo Fernández anota el 3-2 para dar la vuelta a Egipto
items.summary: El mediocampista remató de cabeza en el área tras una contra letal. La selección campeona del mundo dio la vuelta al partido que ganaba 2-0 en los Octavos de Final de la Copa del Mundo.

items.title: Items left behind Cracker Barrel lead to arrest in 1985 death of a salesman
items.summary: Items discarded behind a Cracker Barrel restaurant in Georgia played a key role in helping Ohio prosecutors secure an arrest in connection with the killing of a traveling salesman more than 40 years ago, authorities said Monday.

items.title: International Olympic Committee lifts Russia suspension ahead of 2028 LA Games
items.summary: The International Olympic Committee advised sports bodies to end a three-year program vetting Russians for neutral status ahead of qualifying events for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics.

items.title: 'The building remains unstable': Mamdani briefs on high-rise with buckling beams
items.summary: New York City officials said that a 37-story Manhattan building remains unstable after two structural support beams were reported to be buckling, prompting an emergency response. Mayor Zohran Mamdani said stabilization work would begin as soon as the floor is deemed safe.

items.title: Former mayor of Mississippi's capital city pleads guilty in bribery scheme
items.summary: The former mayor of Mississippi’s capital city and a former City Council president have pleaded guilty in a bribery scheme

items.title: New lawsuit alleges U.S. shared asylum application details with Iran
items.summary: A lawsuit alleges the Trump administration is divulging details of Iranian asylum seekers to the government of Iran.

items.title: Lawsuit says US illegally shared confidential information on Iranian asylum seekers with Iran
items.summary: A lawsuit alleges that President Donald Trump's immigration agencies have shared confidential information about Iranian asylum seekers with the Iranian government

items.title: Trump talks Ukraine-Russia and F-35 jets sale in meeting with Turkey’s Erdoğan
items.summary: President Donald Trump met with Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan to kick off the NATO summit. NBC News’ Monica Alba reports that Trump addressed the sale of F-35 jets to Turkey and reaching a conclusion in the Russia-Ukraine war.

items.title: A toddler was found in a pool and declared dead. He's alive and his parents could be charged
items.summary: Police say an Arizona toddler discovered in a backyard pool in February was declared dead at a hospital but later discovered to be alive

items.title: There's no treatment designed for the Ebola strain ravaging DRC. But now there's hope
items.summary: The strain of the virus that's responsible for the current outbreak has no specific treatments or preventive measures. Three new clinical trials could provide possible breakthroughs.

items.title: After his suspension was lifted, Balogun has little impact in US World Cup loss
items.summary: Folarin Balogun’s presence on the field for the United States against Belgium had a seismic impact on the world of soccer, but he ultimately played a forgettable role in the Americans’ 4-1 loss in the World Cup round of 16

items.title: Woman accuses Graham Platner of sexual assault. And, NATO summit begins
items.summary: Graham Platner is facing calls to end his bid for U.S. Senate in Maine after a woman accused him of sexual assault. And, Trump made his dissatisfaction with NATO clear ahead of its summit this week.

items.title: Balogun starts for US against Belgium after suspension lifted
items.summary: The United States’ hopes for a deep World Cup run at home ended when Charles De Ketelaere scored twice and assisted on another goal, helping Belgium expose the Americans’ defensive liabilities in a 4-1 win Monday night that earned a quarterfinal berth

items.title: Graham Platner faces calls to leave Maine Senate race after sexual assault allegation
items.summary: Graham Platner is facing growing pressure to end his campaign for U.S. Senate in Maine after Politico reported an allegation of sexual assault. Platner has denied the claim.

items.title: Democratic strategist says transparency is Platner's 'only path forward'
items.summary: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Democratic strategist Joel Payne about prominent Democrats on the national and state level urging Graham Platner to withdraw from the Maine Senate race.

items.title: Meet 'Project 2029' — and its war on the annoyance economy
items.summary: Remember Project 2025? Democrats are building their own governing blueprint, and one proposal takes aim at the "annoyance economy": robocalls, endless hold times, hidden fees and other everyday frustrations.

items.title: As climate change damages streets and highways, the road ahead may be expensive
items.summary: Heat waves are becoming more common and intense as a result of climate change — and roads are suffering as a result. Are the nation's roads up to meeting the challenge of a warmer, wetter future?

items.summary: The Trump administration plans to open a 528-bed holding center for migrant families and unaccompanied children awaiting removal from the U.S., putting it next to a Louisiana airfield that has become the nation’s largest hub of deportation flights

items.summary: A handful of countries in the OPEC+ oil-producing alliance are planning to increase their outputs modestly next month

items.summary: President Donald Trump plans to meet with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa while attending the upcoming NATO summit in Turkey

items.title: New Year's Eve ball drops in Times Square for America's 250th
items.summary: The New Year's Eve ball in Times Square has dropped for America's 250th anniversary, making this the first time the ball has dropped outside of a New Year's celebration.

items.title: How to treat and prevent ingrown hair at home
items.summary: How to get rid of ingrown hairs with products from Topicals, Fur, Mario Badescu and more. Here's why dermatologists say you shouldn't pop razor bumps.

items.title: GOP prepared to block vote to replace Feinstein on Senate Judiciary
items.summary: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a floor vote on the issue this afternoon, which Republicans are expected to block.

items.title: Supreme Court seems sympathetic to postal worker who didn't work Sundays in dispute over religious accommodations
items.summary: The Supreme Court seemed to side with a former mail carrier, an evangelical Christian, who says the US Postal Service failed to accommodate his request to not work on Sundays.

items.title: Anti-abortion doctors urge Supreme Court to keep mifepristone restrictions in place
items.summary: A group of doctors opposed to abortion asked the Supreme Court Tuesday to restrict access to a key medication abortion drug while other legal challenges play out, as Wednesday night's deadline for the court to act approaches.

items.title: McCarthy makes plea for Republicans to back debt ceiling plan
items.summary: Speaker Kevin McCarthy made a plea to House Republicans during a closed-door meeting Tuesday morning to back his debt ceiling plan, telling them that although it doesn't have to include everything they want, it will help get him to the negotiating table with President Joe Biden.

items.title: Fulton County, Georgia, jail leadership resigns after inmate's death and accusations of unsanitary conditions
items.summary: Three officials at the Fulton County, Georgia, jail have stepped down amid an investigation into the death of an inmate whose family said was housed in a filthy, bug-infested cell that "was not fit for a diseased animal."

items.title: China's economy is off to a solid start, rising 4.5% in Q1 2023
items.summary: China's economy is off to a solid start in 2023 following its emergence from three years of strict pandemic restrictions.

items.title: He was free for 2 years. Now Crosley Green is back in prison for a crime he says he didn't commit
items.summary: A Florida man who served three decades behind bars for a murder he says he didn't commit returned to prison Monday after spending the past two years building a life outside prison walls.

items.title: Family of victim and survivors of Indianapolis FedEx mass shooting file lawsuit against gun magazine manufacturer and distributors
items.summary: The family of a victim and several survivors of a mass shooting at a FedEx facility in Indianapolis filed a lawsuit against companies involved in the manufacturing, marketing and sale of the high capacity magazine used by the gunman who killed 8 people and injured several others in 2021.

items.title: McDonald's is upgrading its burgers
items.summary: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.

items.title: Google-parent stock drops on fears it could lose search market share to AI-powered rivals
items.summary: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft's Bing.

items.title: Tennessee Air National Guardsman applied to be a hitman online, the FBI says. It was a spoof website and now he's facing charges
items.summary: A Tennessee Air National Guardsman is facing charges after applying to be a hitman on a spoof "rent-a-hitman" website, according to the Department of Justice.
